items.name: Dice set
items.description: This item encompasses a wide range of game pieces, including dice and decks of cards (for games such as Three-Dragon Ante). A few common examples appear on the Tools table, but other kinds of gaming sets exist. If you are proficient with a gaming set, you can add your proficiency bonus to ability checks you make to play a game with that set. Each type of gaming set requires a separate proficiency.

items.name: Pouch
items.description: A cloth or leather pouch can hold up to 20 sling bullets or 50 blowgun needles, among other things. A compartmentalized pouch for holding spell components is called a component pouch (described earlier in this section).

traits: I'm full of inspiring and cautionary tales from my military experience relevant to almost every combat situation.
I'm haunted by memories of war. I can't get the images of violence out of my mind.
I've lost too many friends, and I'm slow to make new ones.
I can stare down a hell hound without flinching.
I enjoy being strong and like breaking things.
I have a crude sense of humor.
I face problems head-on. A simple, direct solution is the best path to success.
ideals: Greater Good. Our lot is to lay down our lives in defense of others. (Good)
Responsibility. I do what must be done and follow orders, even if I don't like them. (Lawful)
Independence. When people follow orders blindly, they embrace a kind of tyranny. (Chaotic)
Might. In life as in war, the stronger force prevails. (Evil)
Live and Let Live. Ideals aren't worth killing over or going to war for. (Neutral)
Nation. My city, nation, or people are all that matter. (Any)
bonds: I would still lay down my life for the people I served with.
Someone saved my life on the battlefield. To this day, I will never leave a friend behind.
I'll never forget the crushing defeat my company suffered or the enemies who dealt it.
Those who fight beside me are those worth dying for.
I fight for those who cannot fight for themselves.
flaws: The monstrous enemy we faced in battle still leaves me quivering with fear.
I have little respect for anyone who is not a proven warrior.
I made a terrible mistake in battle that cost many lives, and I would do anything to keep that mistake secret.
My hatred of my enemies is blind and unreasoning.
I obey the law, even if the law causes misery.
I'd rather eat my armor than admit when I'm wrong.

activeQuests.title: The First Step
activeQuests.description: You awake in a strange clearing. The air is fresh, but the silence is heavy. You must get your bearings.

objectives.description: Master the Booklet: View all pages (Character, Map, Quests, Equipment, Codex)
Study Your Gear: Examine an item in your inventory
Begin the Journey: Move to a neighboring hex

storySummary: started their journey as a Human Fighter with the Soldier background.
lastNarrative: You are unconscious and dying. Roll a Death Save!

conversationHistory.content:
Your eyes flutter open to a sky bruised with the pale light of early morning. The air is crisp, carrying the faint scent of dry grass and distant pine. Around you stretches an endless sea of golden plains, the grass whispering in a gentle breeze that seems to carry a promise of movement. To the south, the plains roll on, but a thin line of darker green hints at a jungle's edge, its canopy a tangled silhouette against the horizon. Northward, low hills rise like sleeping giants, their slopes dotted with occasional clumps of scrub. The eastern horizon fades into a dense jungle, its shadows deep and unknowable, while the western sky is dominated by more hills, their outlines softening as they meet the far‑off mist.

A low rumble rolls from the south‑west, growing louder with each passing heartbeat – thunder, distant but unmistakable, heralding an approaching storm that could reshape the land and perhaps reveal paths hidden beneath the grass. You have no recollection of how you came to lie here; the only thing firmly anchored in your mind is your name, TestHero, and a faint echo of a soldier's discipline.

The ground beneath you feels firm, the grass cool against your skin. No one else stirs in the immediate vicinity – the plains are empty, save for the occasional rustle of a wind‑blown stalk.

You might:
1. **Rise and survey** the surrounding terrain more closely, using your soldier’s eye for detail.
2. **Move toward the rumbling thunder**, perhaps to find shelter or a clue about the storm.
3. **Search the ground** where you lie for any objects, tracks, or signs of recent activity.

A sense of wonder tinges the air, but the distant thunder adds a thread of unease, as if the world itself is holding its breath, waiting for the first drop of rain to fall.

codexEntries.title: Goblin: The Cunning Scavenger of the Wilds
codexEntries.content:
**Physical Description**
Goblins stand no taller than a child of the realm, their wiry frames ranging from three to four feet in height. Their skin, a mottled tapestry of greens, browns, and ash, blends seamlessly with forest floor litter and cavern stone. Long, pointed ears swivel at the faintest rustle, while sharp, yellowed teeth jut from a snarling maw. Their eyes, large and bulbous, possess a reflective sheen that grants them superior night vision. A goblin's hands end in claw‑like nails, adept at both delicate picking and brutal slashing.

**Biology**
While little is known of goblin physiology beyond observation, scholars posit that they are a divergent offshoot of the ancient Gnomish line, adapted for a life of opportunistic predation. Their metabolic rate is unusually high; a goblin can subsist on a diet of insects, carrion, and the occasional stolen morsel from larger beasts. A specialized gland in the jaw secretes a mild anticoagulant, allowing them to inflict lingering wounds that bleed profusely—an evolutionary trait that aids in both hunting and intimidation.

**Ecology**
Goblin tribes favor the shadowed fringes of forests, the crumbling ruins of long‑forgotten fortresses, and the dim tunnels of karst caves. They construct crude shelters from twigs, bone, and scavenged metal, often nesting in proximity to larger predators such as worgs or owlbears, whom they bait and ambush. Their societies are loosely hierarchical, led by the most cunning or the strongest—often a shaman who claims communion with the “Dark Mother,” a deity of decay and hidden things. Goblins are renowned for their raiding parties, which strike at caravans, isolated farms, and unwary travelers, then retreat into the underbrush with loot and the occasional captive for ransom or ritual sacrifice.

**Myths & Legends**
Legends speak of the “Goblin King,” a colossal, silver‑scaled monarch who commands swarms of his kin from a throne of broken armor deep beneath the Blackroot Swamp. Some bards claim that goblins are the cursed remnants of a once‑great dwarven clan, transformed by a vengeful earth spirit. Others whisper that goblin blood, when mixed with moonlit water, can reveal the location of hidden treasure—a rumor that has lured many a fool into ambushes.

**Survivor's Tale**
"I thought the night would end with the taste of iron on my tongue. The snarling horde closed in, their eyes like lanterns in the dark. Yet when the moon slipped behind a cloud, the smallest of them—no taller than my own forearm—lunged, teeth bared, and fell flat on the stone. I seized the moment, drove my blade home, and ran. To this day, the echo of their guttural cries haunts my dreams, a reminder that even the lowliest of foes can strike with the ferocity of a storm."
—Eldric Thorne, survivor of the Redridge Goblin Raid
